name: Golden Dawn
shortDescription: The Hermetic Order of the Golden Dawn was a late 19th and early 20th century secret society dedicated to the study and practice of occult arts and Western esotericism.
description: The Hermetic Order of the Golden Dawn was a magical order active in Great Britain during the late 19th and early 20th centuries. It practiced ceremonial magic, incorporating elements from Hermeticism, Kabbalah, Egyptian mythology, Freemasonry, and other esoteric traditions. The Golden Dawn's influence on modern occultism is immense, shaping many subsequent magical orders and traditions, including Wicca and Thelema. It developed a complex system of ritual magic, utilizing symbolism, divination, and spiritual practices aimed at personal enlightenment and spiritual advancement. Members progressed through a series of grades, each associated with specific knowledge and rituals. Key figures associated with the Golden Dawn include William Wynn Westcott, Samuel Liddell MacGregor Mathers, and Aleister Crowley. The order's teachings emphasized the importance of self-initiation and the exploration of inner consciousness through ritual and meditation. The Golden Dawn ultimately fractured due to internal conflicts and power struggles, but its legacy continues to inspire and inform modern magical practices.

sources.title: The Golden Dawn: An Account of the Teachings, Rites, and Ceremonies of the Order
sources.author: Israel Regardie

sources.title: Self-Initiation into the Golden Dawn Tradition
sources.author: Chic Cicero and Sandra Tabatha Cicero
